---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_macro_panel.csv")

  # Variables
  entity_var: "district"
  time_var: "year"        # set "" to skip time filtering entirely
  filter_time: 2025       # a year (works on raw dates/timestamps too); "" = keep all rows
  value_var: "literacy_rate"

  # Baseline: a number (policy target), or "mean" to benchmark against the
  # average across entities — no manual target needed.
  baseline_target: 65

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Standardized Literacy Rate"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Performance Outliers: Deviation Bar Chart"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers and values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# Works on row-level microdata (pre-aggregates to entity means) AND on data
# that is already one-row-per-entity. Time filter understands plain years,
# dates, and raw timestamps alike.
# ==============================================================================
d <- raw_data

# 1. Time filter (optional): build a comparable key from whatever the time
#    column contains — "2025", "2025-11-01", or "2025-11-01T04:36:41Z"
if (nzchar(params$time_var) && nzchar(as.character(params$filter_time))) {
  time_raw <- d[[params$time_var]]
  as_dates <- cerp_parse_date(time_raw, quiet = TRUE)
  time_key <- if (mean(!is.na(as_dates)) > 0.8) {
    as.character(year(as_dates))            # date-like column -> compare by year
  } else {
    str_squish(as.character(time_raw))      # plain years/labels -> compare as text
  }
  d <- d %>% filter(time_key == str_squish(as.character(params$filter_time)))

  if (nrow(d) == 0) stop(
    "No rows remain after filtering '", params$time_var, "' to '",
    params$filter_time, "'. Values present in the data: ",
    paste(sort(unique(time_key)), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# 2. Pre-aggregate: one summary value per entity (mean of all its rows).
#    Harmless when data is already one-row-per-entity.
agg <- d %>%
  mutate(
    Entity = str_to_title(str_squish(as.character(.data[[params$entity_var]]))),
    Value  = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Entity, Value) %>%
  group_by(Entity) %>%
  summarize(Value = mean(Value), n_obs = n(), .groups = "drop")

# 3. Resolve the baseline: fixed policy target, or the grand mean
use_mean_baseline <- identical(tolower(as.character(params$baseline_target)), "mean")
target <- if (use_mean_baseline) mean(agg$Value) else as.numeric(params$baseline_target)

baseline_text <- if (use_mean_baseline) {
  paste0("the average across all ", nrow(agg), " entities (",
         round(target, 1), ")")
} else {
  paste0("the target of ", round(target, 1))
}

# 4. Deviations, sorted so the biggest laggard sits at the bottom
plot_data <- agg %>%
  mutate(
    Entity_Label = str_wrap(Entity, width = 18),
    Deviation = Value - target,
    Performance = ifelse(Deviation >= 0, "Above baseline", "Below baseline")
  ) %>%
  arrange(Deviation) %>%
  mutate(Entity_Label = factor(Entity_Label, levels = unique(Entity_Label)))
```


```{r plot-deviation, fig.height=max(4, 1 + 0.45 * nrow(plot_data))}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

perf_colors <- c(
  "Above baseline" = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),
  "Below baseline" = unname(cerp_cols["accent"])
)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Deviation, y = Entity_Label, fill = Performance)) +

  geom_col(width = 0.7) +

  # Direct labels: the exact +/- gap at the end of each bar
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = ifelse(Deviation > 0,
                     paste0("+", round(Deviation, 1)),
                     round(Deviation, 1)),
      hjust = ifelse(Deviation > 0, -0.25, 1.25)
    ),
    fontface = "bold", size = 3.4, family = cerp_font_caption,
    color = cerp_cols[["text"]]
  ) +

  # The baseline anchor
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = cerp_cols[["text"]], linewidth = 0.9) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = perf_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.18, 0.18))) +

  theme_cerp() +

  # Direct labels replace the x-axis entirely
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold",
                               color = cerp_cols[["text"]])
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste0(params$metric_name, ": Who Is Ahead, Who Is Behind")),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste0( "Gap relative to ", baseline_text, if (nzchar(params$time_var) && nzchar(as.character(params$filter_time))) paste0(", ", params$filter_time) else "", "." )),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else ("Deviation from baseline"),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
